question: What are the main failure modes of memory in LLM agents?

coverage.aspect: retrieval of stale or irrelevant memories
trade-offs between short-term and long-term memory

claim_lines: - **Context pollution**: the agent over-uses its own prior outputs, so earlier errors, hallucinations, or style artifacts get carried forward instead of filtered out. https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.24287

- **Stale / irrelevant retrieval**: memory returns experiences that are outdated, irrelevant, or only superficially similar, which can mislead the current task and compound errors. https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.16067

- **Over-trusting previous agent outputs**: a specific version of the above, where assistant-side history is treated too much like ground truth rather than as optional guidance. https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.24287

- **Retention vs. update trade-off**: keeping enough short-term context helps continuity, but long-term memory also needs pruning, overwriting, and selective forgetting when facts change or memory gets overloaded. https://openreview.net/pdf/b2b137aed07a6b562044eadcff7892eccefd453d.pdf https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.18642

- **Poor memory-quality evaluation**: good systems are now judged with incremental multi-turn tests that separately score retrieval, test-time learning, long-range understanding, and conflict resolution. https://openreview.net/pdf/b2b137aed07a6b562044eadcff7892eccefd453d.pdf
- context pollution — covered: assistant-side history can contaminate later turns and propagate errors/hallucinations. https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.24287

- retrieval of stale or irrelevant memories — covered: stale or misaligned experiences can mislead current tasks. https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.16067 https://openai.github.io/openai-agents-python/sandbox/memory/
- over-trusting previous agent outputs — covered: over-conditioning on prior assistant responses is a documented failure mode. https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.24287

- trade-offs between short-term and long-term memory — covered: preserve useful history, but overwrite outdated facts and selectively forget when needed. https://openreview.net/pdf/b2b137aed07a6b562044eadcff7892eccefd453d.pdf https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.18642
- evaluation methods for memory quality — covered: MemAE-style incremental multi-turn benchmarks and four competency axes. https://openreview.net/pdf/b2b137aed07a6b562044eadcff7892eccefd453d.pdf
